verses.text: If a watchman sees the sword and blows the trumpet, he warns the people. The office defined. God appoints watchmen to sound the alarm of coming danger.
If a watchman sees the sword and blows the trumpet, he warns the people. The office defined. God appoints watchmen to sound the alarm of coming danger.
If a watchman sees the sword and blows the trumpet, he warns the people. The office defined. God appoints watchmen to sound the alarm of coming danger.
He who hears and heeds not bears his own blood; but if the watchman fails to warn, the blood is required of him. Twofold responsibility. Both hearer and herald answer to God.
He who hears and heeds not bears his own blood; but if the watchman fails to warn, the blood is required of him. Twofold responsibility. Both hearer and herald answer to God.
He who hears and heeds not bears his own blood; but if the watchman fails to warn, the blood is required of him. Twofold responsibility. Both hearer and herald answer to God.
'I have set thee a watchman'; to fail to warn the wicked is to share his guilt, but warning delivers thine own soul. The charge renewed. Faithful warning frees the messenger, whatever the response.
'I have set thee a watchman'; to fail to warn the wicked is to share his guilt, but warning delivers thine own soul. The charge renewed. Faithful warning frees the messenger, whatever the response.
'I have set thee a watchman'; to fail to warn the wicked is to share his guilt, but warning delivers thine own soul. The charge renewed. Faithful warning frees the messenger, whatever the response.

When they despair, 'how should we then live?' God answers, 'As I live... I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked; but that the wicked turn... and live.' Mercy's heart. God longs not to destroy but to save.

When they despair, 'how should we then live?' God answers, 'As I live... I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked; but that the wicked turn... and live.' Mercy's heart. God longs not to destroy but to save.
Past righteousness will not save the man who turns to sin and trusts in it. No coasting. A life with God must be a present reality, not a former record.
Past righteousness will not save the man who turns to sin and trusts in it. No coasting. A life with God must be a present reality, not a former record.
When the wicked turns, restores the pledge, and walks in life's statutes, 'he shall surely live.' Repentance restores. The vilest who truly turns finds life from God.
When the wicked turns, restores the pledge, and walks in life's statutes, 'he shall surely live.' Repentance restores. The vilest who truly turns finds life from God.
When the wicked turns, restores the pledge, and walks in life's statutes, 'he shall surely live.' Repentance restores. The vilest who truly turns finds life from God.
They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal,' but it is their way that is unequal; God judges each by his ways. Justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.
They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal,' but it is their way that is unequal; God judges each by his ways. Justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.
They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal,' but it is their way that is unequal; God judges each by his ways. Justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.
They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal,' but it is their way that is unequal; God judges each by his ways. Justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.
A fugitive comes saying, 'The city is smitten,' and the LORD opens Ezekiel's mouth. Silence ends. With the news of fulfilment, the prophet's fuller ministry begins.
A fugitive comes saying, 'The city is smitten,' and the LORD opens Ezekiel's mouth. Silence ends. With the news of fulfilment, the prophet's fuller ministry begins.
Those left in the ruined land claim it as Abraham's heirs while living in sin; they shall not possess it. Empty pedigree. Promise belongs to faith and obedience, not mere descent.
Those left in the ruined land claim it as Abraham's heirs while living in sin; they shall not possess it. Empty pedigree. Promise belongs to faith and obedience, not mere descent.
Those left in the ruined land claim it as Abraham's heirs while living in sin; they shall not possess it. Empty pedigree. Promise belongs to faith and obedience, not mere descent.
Those left in the ruined land claim it as Abraham's heirs while living in sin; they shall not possess it. Empty pedigree. Promise belongs to faith and obedience, not mere descent.
They shall fall by sword, beasts, and plague, and the land be made desolate. Judgment completed. Presumption upon the land brings only deeper ruin.
They shall fall by sword, beasts, and plague, and the land be made desolate. Judgment completed. Presumption upon the land brings only deeper ruin.
They shall fall by sword, beasts, and plague, and the land be made desolate. Judgment completed. Presumption upon the land brings only deeper ruin.
They come to hear him as 'a very lovely song' but do not obey; when it comes to pass, they shall know a prophet was among them. Hearing without doing. The word admired but unheeded still condemns.
